name: Ace of Wands
uprightMeaning: A spark of creative fire. The Ace of Wands signals the birth of passion and purpose — an irresistible impulse to create, begin, or venture forth.
reversedMeaning: Delays, lack of motivation, creative block. The flame flickers but has not yet caught. Nurture it before forcing it.

name: Two of Wands
uprightMeaning: Standing at the threshold. The Two of Wands shows contemplation before action — the map is drawn, the first steps weighed with care.
reversedMeaning: Fear of the unknown, lack of planning. Hesitation at the threshold — or moving without direction.

name: Three of Wands
uprightMeaning: The first results arrive. Three Wands shows momentum — what was planted is beginning to show, and the horizon holds more than expected.
reversedMeaning: Playing too small, lacking vision, delays. Keep watching the horizon.

name: Four of Wands
uprightMeaning: Foundations solidified. The Four of Wands represents the joy of arrival — a foundation built, a community gathered, a home established.
reversedMeaning: Lack of support, instability, conflict within the home or community. The foundations need tending before the celebration.

name: Five of Wands
uprightMeaning: Creative friction. Five Wands depicts friendly or fierce competition — many voices, many visions, all jostling for space. Resolution requires arbitration.
reversedMeaning: Conflict avoidance, release of tension, peace-making. The noise subsides — or all-out war begins.

name: Six of Wands
uprightMeaning: Triumph acknowledged. The Six of Wands shows victory recognized — a public win, an earned reputation, the crowd's cheer.
reversedMeaning: Failure, humiliation, a fall from grace. Fame turned to infamy — or acclaim that was never earned.
symbolism: figure on white horse with six wands

name: Seven of Wands
uprightMeaning: Standing your ground. Seven Wands shows rising to meet opposition — defended positions, earned territory, the determination to not yield.
reversedMeaning: Overwhelmed, giving up. Outnumbered and outmatched — or the wisdom to choose battles carefully.
symbolism: figure defending against six attacking wands

name: Eight of Wands
uprightMeaning: Rapid acceleration. Eight Wands signals swift progress — things in motion, fast decisions, rapid communication, and events cascading.
reversedMeaning: Delays, frustration. Things stuck in transit — or intentional deceleration.

name: Nine of Wands
uprightMeaning: The last leg of a hard journey. Nine Wands shows perseverance — wounds carried but not fallen, resolve hardened by hardship.
reversedMeaning: Exhaustion, fatigue, giving up. Near the end but strength fails — or learned wisdom to stop fighting.
symbolism: figure with one wand raised among eight planted

name: Ten of Wands
uprightMeaning: Loaded to the limit. Ten of Wands shows a burden carried alone — responsibilities that have accumulated, the cost of overcommitment.
reversedMeaning: Doing it all, burnout. Carrying too much alone — or the freedom found in letting go.

name: Page of Wands
uprightMeaning: A creative messenger. The Page of Wands heralds inspiration arriving — a spark of curiosity, an invitation to explore something new.
reversedMeaning: Setbacks, lack of direction, scattered energy. A false start or exciting opportunity that goes nowhere.

name: Knight of Wands
uprightMeaning: Charging forward. The Knight of Wands embodies bold, passionate action — fast, direct, sometimes reckless, always full of fire.
reversedMeaning: Passion, impulsiveness, delays. A fiery plan that combusts — or hasty action that leads nowhere.

name: Queen of Wands
uprightMeaning: Bold and generous leadership. The Queen of Wands commands with warmth and confidence — clear vision, fierce loyalty, untouchable self-assurance.
reversedMeaning: Selfishness, jealousy, demanding. A queen who rules through fear — or a throne left empty.
symbolism: throned queen with sunflower and wands

name: King of Wands
uprightMeaning: Natural-born commander. The King of Wands rules through vision and boldness — strategic, decisive, inspiring others to act with purpose.
reversedMeaning: Impulsiveness, tyranny, arrogance. A king who rules through fear — or cannot decide at all.
symbolism: throne carved with lions and flames
